proposed_hypotheses.text: The binary outcome objective_response is imbalanced (substantially fewer responders than non-responders) in the cohort.
feature_030 is constant across all 50,000 patients and carries no information about objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: objective_response = 1 in 12,011/50,000 = 24.0% of patients. Imbalanced toward non-response, as hypothesized.
feature_030 has a single unique value (0) for all patients. It is dropped from subsequent analyses.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_008 = 1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with feature_008 = 0 (positive main effect, suggesting feature_008 may be a treatment indicator).
Patients with feature_013 = 1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with feature_013 = 0 (negative main effect).
Higher feature_022 values are associated with lower objective_response (negative continuous main effect).

analyses.result_summary: Response rate = 0.361 in feature_008=1 (n=20,076) vs 0.159 in feature_008=0 (n=29,924); chi-square p~0.
Response rate = 0.154 in feature_013=1 (n=27,481) vs 0.346 in feature_013=0 (n=22,519); chi-square p~0.
Spearman rho = -0.152 between feature_022 and objective_response; p ~ 2.6e-255.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_015 = 1 have a lower objective_response rate than feature_015 = 0.
Patients with feature_021 = 1 have a lower objective_response rate than feature_021 = 0.
Patients with feature_027 = 1 have a lower objective_response rate than feature_027 = 0.
Higher feature_001 levels (ordinal 0/1/2) are associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Response: 0.160 at feature_015=1 (n=10,038) vs 0.260 at feature_015=0 (n=39,962); chi-sq p ~ 1.9e-98.
Response: 0.150 at feature_021=1 (n=4,996) vs 0.250 at feature_021=0 (n=45,004); chi-sq p ~ 5.4e-56.
Response: 0.176 at feature_027=1 (n=1,528) vs 0.242 at feature_027=0 (n=48,472); chi-sq p ~ 3.0e-9.
Response: 0.250 at feature_001=0, 0.227 at feature_001=1, 0.181 at feature_001=2 (Spearman rho=-0.081, p~1.9e-73).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Most other features (feature_005, feature_006, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_023, feature_004, feature_010, feature_026, feature_032, feature_028, feature_007, feature_025, feature_018, feature_003, feature_031, feature_016, feature_009, feature_029, feature_012, feature_014) have no marginal association with objective_response at the conventional p<0.05 threshold.
Higher feature_020 is associated with lower objective_response (small negative effect).
Higher feature_024 is associated with lower objective_response (small negative effect).
Higher feature_002 is associated with higher objective_response (small positive effect).

analyses.code: for f in features: spearmanr or chi2 vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Univariate p-values for all listed features ranged from 0.16 to 0.99; none reach significance individually.
Spearman rho = -0.049, p ~ 1.1e-27.
Spearman rho = -0.025, p ~ 3.5e-8.
Spearman rho = +0.020, p ~ 1.2e-5.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_008 functions like a treatment indicator: it is the only binary feature with a large positive marginal association with objective_response (+20 percentage points), so subsequent treatment-effect heterogeneity analyses should use feature_008 as the treatment.

analyses.code: review of univariate effect signs and sizes for all binary features
analyses.result_summary: Among all binary features, only feature_008 shows a large positive marginal effect (+0.20 absolute risk difference). All other binary features with non-trivial effects (feature_013, _015, _021, _027) have negative associations and prevalence consistent with biomarkers/comorbidities. feature_008 is therefore treated as the candidate treatment variable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_008 on objective_response is modified by feature_013: the response benefit of feature_008=1 vs 0 is much larger when feature_013=0 than when feature_013=1 (negative multiplicative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Logit interaction coefficient feature_008:feature_013 = -1.978 (SE 0.046, z=-42.7, p~0). In feature_013=0, response is 0.610 with feature_008=1 vs 0.169 without (+44.0 pp); in feature_013=1, response is 0.158 vs 0.150 (+0.8 pp). feature_013=1 essentially abolishes the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 modifies the feature_008 treatment effect: feature_015=1 abolishes (or substantially reduces) the response benefit of feature_008.
feature_021 modifies the feature_008 treatment effect: feature_021=1 abolishes (or substantially reduces) the response benefit of feature_008.
feature_027 modifies the feature_008 treatment effect: feature_027=1 abolishes (or substantially reduces) the response benefit of feature_008.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_008 * feature_015', data=df).fit() and stratified means
analyses.result_summary: Stratified treatment effect: at feature_015=0, +0.251 absolute risk diff (n=39,962); at feature_015=1, +0.008 (n=10,038). LR chi-sq for interaction = 426.8 on 1 df, p ~ 0.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_008 * feature_021', data=df).fit() and stratified means
analyses.result_summary: Stratified treatment effect: at feature_021=0, +0.225 absolute risk diff (n=45,004); at feature_021=1, +0.006 (n=4,996). LR chi-sq for interaction = 187.5 on 1 df, p ~ 0.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_008 * feature_027', data=df).fit() and stratified means
analyses.result_summary: Stratified treatment effect: at feature_027=0, +0.209 (n=48,472); at feature_027=1, -0.022 (n=1,528). LR chi-sq for interaction = 85.4 on 1 df, p ~ 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Performing a systematic feature_008-by-feature_X interaction screen using likelihood-ratio tests, the dominant treatment-effect modifiers will be feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027, and feature_022; other features will show only small or null interaction signals after multiple-testing considerations.

analyses.code: for each feature f: LR test of logit(y ~ feature_008 + f + feature_008*f) vs logit(y ~ feature_008 + f)
analyses.result_summary: Top interaction LR statistics: feature_013 (LR=1885, df=1), feature_015 (427), feature_022 (348), feature_021 (188), feature_027 (85), feature_001 (19, df=2; p=6.4e-5). All others have LR<6 (p>0.02). Confirms 4 binary modifiers + feature_022 dominate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_022 reduces the response benefit of feature_008: the multiplicative interaction feature_008 * log(feature_022) is negative.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.415 (SE 0.017, p ~ 0). Marginally, response benefit of feature_008 declines from +0.37 in lowest f022 quartile to +0.03 in highest quartile.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (3-level ordinal) is a small but real modifier of feature_008's treatment effect, with the treatment benefit slightly larger at higher feature_001 levels.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction LR=19.3 on 2 df, p=6.4e-5 (joint significant). However, absolute treatment effect remains roughly +0.20 across all 3 levels of feature_001 (0.21 at level 0, 0.20 at level 1, 0.20 at level 2). Modification is on the logit (multiplicative) scale rather than absolute scale and is small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, and feature_027 are mutually independent (pairwise correlations near zero), so each acts as a distinct/non-redundant resistance marker.

analyses.result_summary: All pairwise Pearson correlations among feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, and feature_027 are |r|<0.005. The four markers vary independently in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When all four resistance-marker interactions (feature_008 x feature_013, x feature_015, x feature_021, x feature_027) are entered simultaneously, each remains independently significant, indicating four distinct sources of treatment-effect attenuation rather than one variable acting as proxy for the others.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_008 + f013 + f015 + f021 + f027 + feature_008:f013 + feature_008:f015 + feature_008:f021 + feature_008:f027', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Joint model: feature_008 main effect = +2.58 (logit). Interactions: feature_008:feature_013 = -2.20 (p~0); :feature_015 = -1.61 (p~0); :feature_021 = -1.52 (p~0); :feature_027 = -1.65 (p~0). All four remain highly significant simultaneously.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining clean_no_resist = (feature_013==0 AND feature_015==0 AND feature_021==0 AND feature_027==0): in this subgroup the treatment effect of feature_008 is dramatically larger than in the complementary subgroup (any marker = 1).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_008 * clean_no_resist', data=df).fit() and stratified means
analyses.result_summary: In clean_no_resist=1 (n=15,681; 31% of cohort): response 0.798 with feature_008=1 vs 0.172 without, +0.626 abs. In clean_no_resist=0 (n=34,319): 0.161 vs 0.153, +0.008 abs (chi-sq p=0.056, NS). Logit interaction feature_008:clean_no_resist = +2.885 (SE 0.051, p~0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After conditioning on the four resistance markers, feature_022 (and feature_001) lose their independent treatment-effect-modifier status: their interactions with feature_008 are largely explained by correlation with the resistance markers.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_008 * (feature_013 + feature_015 + feature_021 + feature_027 + log(feature_022) + C(feature_001))', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In the joint model, feature_008:log(feature_022) = -0.007 (p=0.74; lost significance), and feature_008:feature_001 levels are p=0.72 and p=0.13 (lost). The four resistance-marker interactions remain highly significant. So feature_022 and feature_001 modification is largely a marginal artifact of the four binary modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the clean_no_resist subgroup, the treatment effect of feature_008 is approximately constant across feature_022 quartiles (no remaining heterogeneity after conditioning on the four resistance markers).

analyses.result_summary: Within clean subgroup (n=15,681), treatment effect by f022 quartile: Q0 +0.633, Q1 +0.628, Q2 +0.624, Q3 +0.618. Essentially flat at ~+0.63. Confirms residual f022 modification is null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A decision tree fit only to feature_008=1 patients to predict objective_response will surface feature_013, feature_015, feature_021 (and feature_027) as the top splits, mirroring the resistance-marker subgroup structure.

analyses.result_summary: On the treatment arm (n=20,076), root split is feature_013<=0.5; the next splits in the responder branch are feature_015<=0.5 and feature_021<=0.5. The non-responder branches do not produce class=1 predictions. This independently corroborates the resistance-marker subgroup structure derived from the interaction screen.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After accounting for feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027 (and their interactions with feature_008), no other feature in the cohort exhibits a clinically meaningful (>+/-0.05 absolute risk-difference modification) treatment-effect-modifier signal.

analyses.code: Within clean_no_resist=1 subgroup, screen feature_008 x f for all remaining features.
analyses.result_summary: Within clean_no_resist=1, treatment effect is ~+0.626 globally. Stratified by feature_001 (0.626/0.629/0.610), feature_002 quartile (0.62-0.64), feature_005 (0.624/0.629), feature_011 (0.628/0.622), feature_023 (0.625/0.629): no subgroup deviates by more than ~2 percentage points from the overall +63 pp benefit. No additional modifier was found.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among feature_008=0 patients (no treatment), feature_001 and feature_022 remain prognostic for objective_response: higher feature_001 and higher feature_022 each independently predict lower response in the absence of treatment.

analyses.result_summary: In control arm (n=29,924): feature_001 level 1 vs 0 logit = -0.36 (p~0), level 2 vs 0 = -0.77 (p~0). log(feature_022) coef = -0.072 (p~3e-7). Both remain prognostic without treatment, even though they do not robustly modify the *treatment effect* once resistance markers are accounted for.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Allocation of feature_008 is approximately balanced across the four resistance markers (so the heterogeneity finding is not an artifact of confounded treatment allocation).

analyses.code: for m in [feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027]: pd.crosstab(df['feature_008'], df[m], normalize='index')
analyses.result_summary: Conditional probabilities of each modifier given feature_008=1 vs 0 differ by <0.01 absolute in each case (e.g., P(feature_013=1 | feature_008=1)=0.550 vs P(feature_013=1 | feature_008=0)=0.550). Treatment is essentially randomized with respect to the modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within clean_no_resist=1, none of the continuous biomarkers (feature_009, feature_018, feature_022, feature_024, feature_026, feature_029, feature_003) significantly modify the feature_008 treatment effect after Bonferroni correction.

analyses.code: for f in continuous: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_008 * f', data=df[clean_no_resist==1]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All within-clean interaction p-values exceed 0.05 (best = feature_022 p=0.45). After Bonferroni for ~25 tests, no continuous biomarker independently modifies the treatment effect within the clean subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the clean_no_resist=1 subgroup, the odds ratio for objective_response with feature_008=1 vs 0 is at least 15.

analyses.result_summary: Clean subgroup 2x2: feature_008=1 responders=5046, non-resp=1279; feature_008=0 responders=1611, non-resp=7745. OR = (5046*7745)/(1279*1611) = 18.97. 95% CI (logit) ~ (17.4, 20.7). Highly significant (p ~ 0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the clean_no_resist=0 (any-marker-positive) subgroup, the odds ratio for objective_response with feature_008=1 vs 0 is approximately 1 (no treatment benefit).

analyses.result_summary: Resist subgroup: response 0.161 with feature_008=1 (n=13,751) vs 0.153 with feature_008=0 (n=20,568). OR = 1.06, 95% CI (0.99, 1.13), chi-sq p=0.056. Treatment effect is essentially null in this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Increasing the count of resistance markers (number of {feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027} = 1) progressively reduces the treatment effect of feature_008, with the largest drop occurring between count=0 and count=1.

analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect by resist_count: 0 markers +0.626 (n=15,681); 1 marker +0.009 (n=25,372); 2 markers +0.008 (n=8,186); 3 markers -0.022 (n=745); 4 markers -0.091 (n=16). Almost the entire effect modification is captured by the count=0 vs count>=1 contrast; additional markers beyond the first add little further attenuation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no feature in the cohort whose presence amplifies the feature_008 treatment effect beyond the +0.63 observed in the clean subgroup (no 'super-responder' subgroup beyond clean_no_resist=1).

analyses.code: examine treatment effects across single-feature subsets within clean_no_resist=1
analyses.result_summary: Within clean_no_resist=1, the largest subgroup-specific treatment effect is +0.65 (feature_001=0 stratum), only marginally above the overall +0.63. No feature identifies a 'super-responder' subgroup within the already-clean group.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: The treatment feature_008 increases objective_response only in patients who simultaneously have feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0. In this subgroup (~31% of the cohort, n=15,681), feature_008=1 raises response from ~17% to ~80% (absolute risk difference ~+0.63; OR ~19; p~0). Outside this subgroup the treatment effect is approximately zero (absolute risk difference ~+0.01, OR ~1.06, p=0.056). feature_022 and feature_001 are prognostic in the absence of treatment but do not independently modify the treatment effect after conditioning on the four resistance markers.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_008 * clean_no_resist', data=df).fit() with secondary checks above.
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup-specific treatment effect: clean_no_resist=1: +0.626 absolute risk difference (OR 18.97, p~0); clean_no_resist=0: +0.008 (OR 1.06, p=0.056). Logit interaction feature_008:clean_no_resist = +2.885 (z=56.1, p~0). All four binary modifiers remain individually significant in the joint interaction model. This is the best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis for the objective_response outcome.
